title: Memory Reactivation Levels Remain Unaffected by Anticipated Interference
description: This dataset contains EEG data from a memory interference task designed to investigate how anticipated interference affects memory reactivation. Participants learned target objects and performed a recognition task with manipulated interference conditions, where irrelevant novel objects were presented on half the trials during a delay period. EEG was recorded using 64 electrodes at 500 Hz sampling rate. The study examines whether memory reactivation levels are modulated by the expectation of interfering stimuli.
methods_description: Memory interference task with trial structure: space key initiation, fixation (1000-1500 ms), presentation of cued target and novel object, 1400 ms delay with interference objects on half of blocks, probe presentation with vertical object alignment, and response-contingent feedback (1000 ms). EEG preprocessing procedures including artifact rejection, filtering, and epoching are detailed in the associated preprint and OSF repository.
